 {"id":"bd-2ddl","title":"§17.1 Unit Tests: Per-Crate Test Matrix (All 23 Crates)","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_2ddl_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY
Defines the per-crate unit test matrix for all 23 FrankenSQLite crates. Every public function and every non-trivial private function must have at least one #[test]. Trait dependencies are mocked using hand-written mock implementations (not a mocking framework). The spec provides concrete test scenarios for fsqlite-types, fsqlite-vfs, and fsqlite-btree as normative examples; all other crates must follow the same discipline.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS
- **fsqlite-types tests:** SqliteValue comparison semantics (Integer(3) vs Real(3.0) = Equal), SqliteValue text-to-integer coercion, PageNumber zero-rejection, Opcode distinct u8 values for all 190+ variants, serial type round-trip encode/decode for every category.
- **fsqlite-vfs tests:** MemoryVfs write/read 1MB byte-for-byte identity, MemoryVfs truncate verification, UnixVfs create/write/close/reopen/read cycle, UnixVfs delete non-existent file error, UnixVfs concurrent reader consistency.
- **fsqlite-btree tests:** Insert 10K random i64 keys, delete 5K random subset, verify remaining 5K present and sorted via cursor iteration. Insert keys forcing tree depth to 4, verify cursor traversal visits all keys. Overflow page chain for 100KB payload read-back. Freelist page reclamation verified via dbstat-equivalent accounting.
- **Mock Strategy:** Hand-written mock implementations of traits (no mocking framework) to keep tests understandable and deterministic.
- **Coverage Discipline:** Per-crate test modules, each crate's #[test] functions cover public API surface plus non-trivial internal logic.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS
- INV-UT-1: Every public function in every crate MUST have at least one #[test].
- INV-UT-2: Every non-trivial private function MUST have at least one #[test].
- INV-UT-3: Trait dependencies MUST be mocked using hand-written mock implementations, not a mocking framework.
- INV-UT-4: Test names must be descriptive and indicate the behavior being verified.
- INV-UT-5: All 23 crates must have test modules — no crate is exempt.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS
- `test_sqlite_value_integer_real_comparison_equal`: SqliteValue::Integer(3) compared with SqliteValue::Real(3.0) returns Equal.
- `test_sqlite_value_text_to_integer_coercion`: SqliteValue::Text("123") coerced to Integer context yields SqliteValue::Integer(123).
- `test_page_number_zero_rejected`: PageNumber::new(0) returns Err.
- `test_opcode_distinct_u8_values`: Collect u8 values of all 190+ Opcode variants into a set; set size equals variant count (no collisions).
- `test_serial_type_roundtrip_all_categories`: For each serial type category, encode then decode and verify identity.
- `test_memory_vfs_write_read_1mb`: Write 1MB to MemoryVfs, read back, assert byte-for-byte identity.
- `test_memory_vfs_truncate`: Write 1MB, truncate to 512KB, verify file_size() == 512KB and read returns only 512KB.
- `test_unix_vfs_create_write_close_reopen_read`: Create file in temp dir, write data, close, reopen, read back and verify.
- `test_unix_vfs_delete_nonexistent`: Delete non-existent file via UnixVfs, verify appropriate error returned.
- `test_unix_vfs_concurrent_readers`: Two readers on same file see consistent data.
- `test_btree_insert_delete_sorted_order`: Insert 10K random i64 keys, delete 5K random subset, verify remaining 5K present and sorted via cursor.
- `test_btree_depth_4_cursor_traversal`: Insert keys forcing depth 4, verify cursor visits all keys.
- `test_btree_overflow_page_chain_100kb`: Insert 100KB payload, verify complete read-back through overflow chain.
- `test_btree_freelist_reclamation`: Delete records, verify freed pages appear on freelist via dbstat-equivalent check.

- test_e2e_bd_2ddl_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.
For each of the 23 crates, run `cargo test -p <crate_name>` and verify all tests pass. Aggregate test count per crate and verify minimum coverage threshold. Run the full workspace `cargo test --workspace` and verify zero failures. Validate that every public function listed in each crate's API has a corresponding test (automated via a coverage audit script).

## Logging Requirements

- INFO: per-crate test matrix summary: `crate`, `unit`, `prop`, `conformance`, `fuzz`.
- WARN: missing test category for any crate.


## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA
- All 23 crates have #[test] modules with comprehensive coverage of public API.
- fsqlite-types: SqliteValue comparison, coercion, PageNumber validation, Opcode uniqueness, and serial type round-trips all pass.
- fsqlite-vfs: MemoryVfs and UnixVfs tests pass including concurrent reader test.
- fsqlite-btree: 10K insert/delete/verify, depth-4 traversal, overflow chain, and freelist reclamation tests all pass.
- Hand-written mocks used for all trait dependencies (no mocking framework).
- `cargo test --workspace` passes with zero failures.
- No crate has zero tests.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:04:51.521821180Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T21:11:56.131950235Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T21:11:56.131925569Z","close_reason":"Required §17.1 types/vfs/btree matrix tests implemented and bd_2ddl compliance harness green","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","depends_on_id":"bd-1fpm","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T11:03:26.274620084Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:41.426694377Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","depends_on_id":"bd-21r0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:38:05.621741055Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":93,"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.1 Unit Tests Per-Crate (from P2 bd-1p0j)

Every public + non-trivial private function has >=1 #[test]. Hand-written mocks (no framework).

**Concrete scenarios by crate:**
- fsqlite-types: SqliteValue comparison (Int/Real equality), coercion, PageNumber reject 0, Opcode distinct u8 values, serial type round-trip.
- fsqlite-vfs: MemoryVfs write 1MB + read back, truncate, UnixVfs create/write/close/reopen, delete non-existent error, concurrent readers.
- fsqlite-btree: 10K random keys insert + 5K delete + verify sorted order. Depth 4 traversal. 100KB overflow payload. Freelist reclaim.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:23:02Z"},{"id":154,"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.1 Unit Tests: Per-Crate Test Matrix

### Spec Content (Lines 16304-16333)

Every public function and every non-trivial private function has at least one #[test]. Trait dependencies are mocked using hand-written mock implementations (not a mocking framework) to keep tests understandable.

**Concrete test scenarios by crate:**

**fsqlite-types:**
- SqliteValue: comparison between Integer(3) and Real(3.0) returns Equal
- SqliteValue: Text("123") coerced to Integer context yields Integer(123)
- PageNumber: construction from 0 returns error
- Opcode: all 190+ variants have distinct u8 values
- Serial type: round-trip encode/decode for every serial type category

**fsqlite-vfs:**
- MemoryVfs: write 1MB, read back, verify byte-for-byte identity
- MemoryVfs: truncate from 1MB to 512KB, verify file_size and read
- UnixVfs: create in temp directory, write, close, reopen, read back
- UnixVfs: delete non-existent file returns appropriate error
- UnixVfs: two concurrent readers on same file see consistent data

**fsqlite-btree:**
- Insert 10K random i64 keys, delete 5K random subset, verify remaining 5K present and in sorted order via cursor iteration
- Insert keys forcing tree depth to 4, verify cursor traversal visits all keys
- Overflow page chain for 100KB payload, read back complete
- Freelist reclaims pages, verify via dbstat-equivalent accounting

**Key principle:** Hand-written mocks (not mocking frameworks) for trait dependencies, ensuring test readability.

### Unit Tests Required
1. test_sqlite_value_integer_real_comparison: Integer(3) vs Real(3.0) returns Equal
2. test_sqlite_value_text_coercion: Text("123") to Integer context yields Integer(123)
3. test_page_number_zero_error: PageNumber::new(0) returns error
4. test_opcode_190_distinct_values: All 190+ Opcode variants have distinct u8 values
5. test_serial_type_all_categories_roundtrip: Encode/decode round-trip for each serial type category (NULL, int8, int16, int24, int32, int48, int64, float64, const-0, const-1, text, blob)
6. test_memory_vfs_write_1mb_readback: Write 1MB, read back, byte-for-byte identity
7. test_memory_vfs_truncate_verify: Truncate 1MB to 512KB, check file_size and read
8. test_unix_vfs_temp_roundtrip: Create in temp dir, write, close, reopen, read back
9. test_unix_vfs_delete_nonexistent_error: Delete non-existent returns appropriate error
10. test_unix_vfs_concurrent_readers: Two concurrent readers on same file see consistent data
11. test_btree_10k_insert_5k_delete: Insert 10K random keys, delete 5K, verify remaining sorted
12. test_btree_depth_4_cursor: Force tree depth to 4, cursor visits all keys
13. test_btree_overflow_100kb: 100KB payload overflow chain, read back complete
14. test_btree_freelist_reclaim_accounting: Freed pages tracked and reused, dbstat verification
15. test_mock_vfs_for_btree: Hand-written mock Vfs used in B-tree tests (no mocking framework)
16. test_mock_pager_for_vdbe: Hand-written mock Pager used in VDBE tests

### E2E Test
End-to-end validation: Run the full per-crate unit test matrix across all crates (fsqlite-types, fsqlite-vfs, fsqlite-btree) in a single cargo test invocation. Verify that all hand-written mock implementations correctly simulate trait behavior by comparing results against real implementations. Ensure every public function has at least one test (code coverage gate).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:13Z"},{"id":901,"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"SageRiver sub-lane update: added direct  crate unit coverage in  for public API surface via re-export path. New tests: connection open/path, expression query, query_with_params, query_row first-row behavior, query_row empty-result error (), and execute row-count behavior. This advances §17.1 per-crate matrix by giving  explicit tests instead of only transitive coverage from core. Validation: UBS on  reports critical=0. Full  is currently blocked by unrelated compile failures in  (/ missing, clone_pool mismatch), outside this lane.","created_at":"2026-02-09T15:50:44Z"},{"id":902,"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Correction from SageRiver: completed fsqlite crate unit-test sub-lane in crates/fsqlite/src/lib.rs with tests for open/path, query, query_with_params, query_row success, query_row empty-result error, and execute row-count. This improves direct per-crate coverage for fsqlite. Full cargo test -p fsqlite remains blocked by unrelated fsqlite-pager compile failures (missing SimpleTransaction/PagerRef and clone_pool mismatch) outside this lane.","created_at":"2026-02-09T15:50:55Z"},{"id":914,"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"CloudyPond claiming focused bd-2ddl sub-slice in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_2ddl_test_matrix_compliance.rs for strict-clippy cleanup and compliance gate stabilization.","created_at":"2026-02-09T16:29:01Z"},{"id":952,"issue_id":"bd-2ddl","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RainyBay lane evidence update: implemented/validated required §17.1 test identifiers in touched crates.\n\nAdded tests:\n- crates/fsqlite-types/src/value.rs: test_sqlite_value_integer_real_comparison_equal, test_sqlite_value_text_to_integer_coercion\n- crates/fsqlite-types/src/lib.rs: test_page_number_zero_rejected\n- crates/fsqlite-types/src/opcode.rs: test_opcode_distinct_u8_values\n- crates/fsqlite-types/src/record.rs: test_serial_type_roundtrip_all_categories\n- crates/fsqlite-btree/src/cursor.rs: test_btree_insert_delete_sorted_order, test_btree_depth_4_cursor_traversal, test_btree_overflow_page_chain_100kb\n- crates/fsqlite-btree/src/freelist.rs: test_btree_freelist_reclamation\n\nPresence check: all required identifiers for types/vfs/btree found via ripgrep across crates.\n\nValidation evidence:\n- cargo test -p fsqlite-types -p fsqlite-vfs -p fsqlite-btree -- --nocapture  (all pass)\n- cargo test -p fsqlite-harness --test bd_2ddl_test_matrix_compliance -- --nocapture  (5/5 pass)\n\nNote: global workspace clippy/fmt are currently affected by unrelated concurrent in-flight edits outside this lane.","created_at":"2026-02-09T21:11:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2de5","title":"§17.5-17.9 E-Process Monitoring + Fuzz + Conformance + Perf Regression + Isomorphism","description":"STATUS: CLOSED (superseded rollup)

This bead is a consolidated/rolled-up version of §17.5-§17.9 created during an earlier spec-to-beads conversion pass.

DO NOT implement from this rollup directly.

Use these replacement beads instead (plan-of-record):
- bd-1cx0 — §17.5 Runtime Invariant Monitoring: E-Processes for Anytime-Valid Checks
- bd-1ft5 — §17.6 Fuzz Test Specifications: SQL Parser + Record Format + Wire Protocol Fuzzing
- bd-3d5b — §17.7 Conformance Testing: Golden-File Suite Against C sqlite3 Oracle
- bd-3cl3 — §17.8 Performance Regression Detection: Criterion + Split Conformal + E-Process
- bd-3uoj — §17.9 Isomorphism Proof Template: Required for Optimization Correctness

NOTE: The original consolidated spec extract for this rollup lives in the bead comments.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:53.788855791Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T17:35:14.741143170Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T06:23:52.541472723Z","close_reason":"Content merged into bd-1cx0 (§17.5), bd-1ft5 (§17.6), bd-3d5b (§17.7), bd-3cl3 (§17.8), bd-3uoj (§17.9)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2de5","depends_on_id":"bd-1p0j","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:17:14.039990530Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2de5","depends_on_id":"bd-21c","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:09:41.700164979Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":32,"issue_id":"bd-2de5","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## §17.5-17.9 E-Process Monitoring + Fuzz Tests + Conformance Testing + Performance + Isomorphism Proofs

### E-Process Monitoring (§17.5)
INV-1 (Monotonicity), INV-2 (Lock Exclusivity), INV-3 (Version Chain Order), INV-4 (Write Set Consistency), INV-5 (Snapshot Stability), INV-6 (Commit Atomicity), INV-7 (Serialized Mode Exclusivity) = hard invariants. INV-SSI-FP = statistical.

**Recommendation:** debug_assert! for INV-1..7 (zero false-alarm, zero overhead in release, immediate stack trace). E-processes reserved for INV-SSI-FP and rate-based metrics where sequential hypothesis testing adds value.

### Fuzz Tests (§17.6)
**SQL parser fuzz:** Arbitrary bytes → parse() must not panic or loop.
**Grammar-based SQL fuzzing:** `arbitrary` crate for structured SQL. Execute, verify no panic/corruption, PRAGMA integrity_check if Ok.
**Other targets:** record_decoder, btree_page_decoder, wal_frame_decoder, json_parser, raptorq_decoder (correct output or error, never silent corruption).

### Conformance Testing (§17.7)
**Principle:** Conformance from Phase 1 (not Phase 9). "We change HOW, not WHAT."
**Oracle:** C SQLite 3.52.0 from legacy_sqlite_code/. In-process or runner binary.

**Mode matrix:** Every case declares compatibility/native/both modes. Default = both. Mode-only cases require explicit reason. CI: output MUST match Oracle per mode. Cross-mode outputs MUST match each other. Fixture annotation: fsqlite_modes + fsqlite_modes_reason.

**Categories:** DDL (100+), DML (200+), Expressions (150+), Functions (200+), Transactions (100+), Edge cases (100+), Extensions (100+), Concurrency regression.

**What we compare:** Result rows, type affinity, error code + extended, changes()/total_changes(), last_insert_rowid(), transaction boundary effects.

**JSON fixture format:** name, fsqlite_modes, steps (open/exec/query with expect).
**SLT ingestion:** SQLLogicTest files for broad coverage.
**Normalization:** Unordered results as multisets. Float: exact strings (default) or tolerance. Errors: compare codes not messages.
**Golden output discipline:** Every change preserves golden outputs unless intentional divergence documented.

### Performance Regression Detection (§17.8)
**Discipline:** Baseline → Profile → Prove behavior unchanged → Implement → Re-measure. No "vibes" optimization.

**Required benchmarks:** Micro: page read path, delta apply, SSI overhead, RaptorQ encode/decode, coded index lookup. Macro: multi-writer scaling, conflict rate vs M2_hat, scan vs random (ARC vs LRU), replication convergence.

**Statistical methodology (split conformal + e-process, distribution-free):**
1. Baseline: N_base ≥ ceil(M/alpha_total) seeds. Canonical: 1200 (M=12, alpha=0.01). Relaxed: 120.
2. Split conformal "no regression" bound U_alpha.
3. Candidate: N_cand ≥ 10 seeds.
4. Gate: cand_stat > U_alpha = regression.
5. Optional: e-process anytime-valid monitor.
6. Multiple testing: Bonferroni (alpha/M) or alpha-investing.

**Extreme Optimization Loop (§17.8.1):** BASELINE → PROFILE → PROVE → IMPLEMENT (one lever) → VERIFY → REPEAT.
**Deterministic Measurement (§17.8.2):** Fixed seed, params, env, git_sha. Schedule fingerprint for concurrent scenarios.
**Opportunity Matrix (§17.8.3):** Score = (Impact × Confidence) / Effort. Gate: Score ≥ 2.0. No hotspot = Score 0.
**Baseline Artifacts (§17.8.4):** baselines/ directory. Perf smoke report JSON schema.
**Profiling Cookbook (§17.8.5):** flamegraph, hyperfine, heaptrack, strace. Required metadata.
**Golden Checksums (§17.8.6):** sha256sum behavior lock for perf-only changes.

### Isomorphism Proof Template (§17.9)
Required for every performance optimization PR:
- Ordering preserved, tie-breaking unchanged, float behavior, RNG seeds, oracle fixtures PASS.
","created_at":"2026-02-08T05:16:53Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2e3s","title":"Build corruption injection framework (byte/page/sector-level)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:56.323235009Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T05:01:43.055907619Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T05:01:43.055886420Z","close_reason":"Corruption injection framework already fully implemented in corruption.rs: 7 CorruptionPattern variants (BitFlip, PageZero, RandomOverwrite, PagePartialCorrupt, HeaderZero, WalFrameCorrupt, SidecarCorrupt), CorruptionInjector with golden-path safety, CorruptionReport with SHA-256, inject_many(), 15 tests all passing.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2e3s","depends_on_id":"bd-34f1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:09.308936300Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2e3s","depends_on_id":"bd-3cbw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:09.092226164Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1105,"issue_id":"bd-2e3s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Implementation matches the task scope and title
- All unit tests pass; code compiles without warnings under clippy pedantic+nursery
- Integration with existing harness framework verified
- No unsafe code, no panics in normal operation
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:21:39Z"},{"id":1121,"issue_id":"bd-2e3s","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Task: Build corruption injection framework (byte/page/sector-level)

### What
Create a Rust library for precisely injecting corruption into SQLite database files at various granularities. This is the core utility used by all corruption recovery demos.

### Design
```rust
pub struct CorruptionInjector {
    path: PathBuf,
}

pub enum CorruptionPattern {
    /// Flip a single bit at a specific byte offset
    BitFlip { byte_offset: u64, bit_position: u8 },
    /// Zero out an entire 4096-byte page
    PageZero { page_number: u32 },
    /// Overwrite N bytes at offset with random data (seeded)
    RandomOverwrite { offset: u64, length: usize, seed: u64 },
    /// Overwrite N bytes within a specific page
    PagePartialCorrupt { page_number: u32, offset_within_page: u16, length: u16, seed: u64 },
    /// Zero out the 100-byte database header
    HeaderZero,
    /// Corrupt specific WAL frames
    WalFrameCorrupt { frame_numbers: Vec<u32>, seed: u64 },
    /// Corrupt FEC sidecar file
    SidecarCorrupt { offset: u64, length: usize, seed: u64 },
}

impl CorruptionInjector {
    pub fn new(path: PathBuf) -> Self { ... }

    /// Apply a corruption pattern to the file
    pub fn inject(&self, pattern: &CorruptionPattern) -> Result<CorruptionReport> { ... }

    /// Apply multiple corruption patterns
    pub fn inject_many(&self, patterns: &[CorruptionPattern]) -> Result<Vec<CorruptionReport>> { ... }
}

pub struct CorruptionReport {
    pub pattern: CorruptionPattern,
    pub affected_bytes: u64,
    pub affected_pages: Vec<u32>,
    pub original_checksum: String,  // SHA-256 of affected region before corruption
}
```

### Safety Guarantees
- NEVER operates on the original file — caller must pass a working copy
- Returns a CorruptionReport documenting exactly what was changed
- Captures original bytes before corruption (for verification)
- Deterministic: same seed produces same corruption pattern

### Page Math
- Page size: 4096 bytes (standard SQLite)
- Page 1 starts at offset 0 (contains header)
- Page N starts at offset (N-1) * 4096
- Total pages: file_size / page_size

### WAL Frame Math
- WAL header: 32 bytes
- Frame header: 24 bytes
- Frame data: page_size bytes
- Frame N starts at: 32 + N * (24 + page_size)

### Acceptance Criteria
- All CorruptionPattern variants implemented
- Works on any SQLite database file
- CorruptionReport captures original state
- Deterministic injection with seeded RNG
- Unit tests verify each pattern type
- Does NOT operate on files in golden/ directory (safety check)
","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:23:10Z"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2ez0","title":"Windows VFS interop stabilization: confirm non-flaky CI with consecutive green runs","description":"Post-checkpoint hardening for Windows-only verification lane. Windows VFS Interop workflow has recent failures (runs 21846570497, 21846577009, 21846624787, 21846664276, 21846829669, 21846918082) and one latest green run (21847000388). We need confidence this lane is now stable and non-flaky.\n\nScope:\n- Trigger and monitor at least two additional Windows VFS Interop runs on main.\n- If failures recur, identify root cause from logs and patch tests/code/workflow accordingly.\n- If runs are green, capture evidence and summarize residual risk.\n\nAcceptance Criteria:\n- Two consecutive Windows VFS Interop runs complete successfully on main after latest fixes.\n- Failure mode analysis is documented if any run fails.\n- Bead comments include run URLs and pass/fail outcomes.\n- Follow-up bead(s) created for any unresolved failures.","notes":"Validation evidence (main branch):\n- Success: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite/actions/runs/21847093999\n- Success: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite/actions/runs/21847133760\nAdditional nearby success in same window:\n- Success: https://github.com/Dicklesworthstone/frankensqlite/actions/runs/21847096294\n\nResult: at least two consecutive green Windows VFS Interop runs after latest fixes;  {"id":"bd-2fas","title":"§11.9 WAL Checksum Chain Recovery + Rollback Journal Stride-200 Checksum","description":"E2E Test:
- test_e2e_bd_2fas_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

## SUMMARY

Specifies WAL checksum chain recovery and rollback journal stride-200 checksum validation. The WAL uses a custom double-accumulator checksum (NOT CRC-32, NOT xxHash) applied in a cumulative chain: WAL header checksum seeds the first frame, each subsequent frame's checksum covers itself and all prior frames. Recovery walks frames sequentially; the first frame failing checksum or salt match terminates the valid WAL prefix. The rollback journal uses a stride-200 byte-sampling checksum: `nonce + sum(data[page_size - 200*k])` for k where offset > 0, with data[0] NEVER sampled.

## KEY DATA STRUCTURES / ALGORITHMS

- **WAL checksum chain** (per section 7.1 wal_checksum function):
  1. WAL header checksum: `wal_checksum(header_bytes[0..24], 0, 0, big_end_cksum)` stored at header bytes 24..32.
  2. First frame: `wal_checksum(frame_header[0..8] ++ page_data, hdr_cksum1, hdr_cksum2, big_end_cksum)` stored at frame header bytes 16..24. Only first 8 bytes of frame header checksummed (NOT bytes 8..16 containing salt).
  3. Subsequent frames: use previous frame's (cksum1, cksum2) as seed. Cumulative chain.
- **WAL recovery validation**: Walk frames sequentially. Frame valid iff: (a) recomputed checksum matches stored values, AND (b) salt values match WAL header salt. First failing frame terminates valid prefix.
- **Rollback journal checksum**: `nonce + data[page_size-200] + data[page_size-400] + ... + data[k]` where k is smallest value > 0 in the arithmetic sequence. Loop condition `while(i > 0)` means data[0] is NEVER sampled. Each data[i] reads a single u8 byte accumulated into u32 sum.
- **Rollback journal stride calculation**: For 4096-byte pages: 20 bytes sampled at offsets 3896, 3696, ..., 296, 96. Count = (3896 - 96) / 200 + 1 = 20.

## NORMATIVE INVARIANTS

1. WAL checksum is a custom double-accumulator (NOT CRC-32, NOT xxHash); canonical implementation in section 7.1.
2. WAL header checksum covers only bytes 0..24 (first 24 bytes), stored at bytes 24..32.
3. Frame checksum covers frame_header[0..8] (NOT bytes 8..16 containing salt) concatenated with full page data.
4. Checksum chain is cumulative: each frame's checksum depends on all prior frames.
5. WAL recovery stops at first frame with mismatched checksum OR mismatched salt.
6. Rollback journal checksum stride is exactly 200 bytes, starting from `page_size - 200`.
7. Rollback journal checksum NEVER samples data[0] (loop condition is `while(i > 0)`, strictly positive).
8. Rollback journal nonce is included in checksum sum as initial value.

## UNIT TEST REQUIREMENTS

1. `test_wal_header_checksum` -- Compute wal_checksum over 24-byte header with seed (0,0); verify matches known good values.
2. `test_wal_first_frame_checksum` -- First frame checksum seeds from header checksum; covers frame_header[0..8] + page_data.
3. `test_wal_chain_three_frames` -- Three-frame WAL: each frame's checksum correctly chains from previous.
4. `test_wal_frame_salt_validation` -- Frame with correct checksum but wrong salt is rejected.
5. `test_wal_recovery_valid_prefix` -- WAL with 5 valid frames and 1 corrupt frame recovers exactly 5 frames.
6. `test_wal_recovery_first_frame_corrupt` -- Corrupt first frame: recovery yields 0 valid frames.
7. `test_wal_frame_header_checksum_excludes_salt` -- Verify only frame_header[0..8] is checksummed, not bytes 8..16.
8. `test_rollback_journal_checksum_stride200` -- For 4096-byte page: verify 20 bytes sampled at correct offsets (3896, 3696, ..., 96).
9. `test_rollback_journal_checksum_never_samples_zero` -- Verify data[0] is never included in checksum regardless of page size.
10. `test_rollback_journal_checksum_nonce` -- Nonce value correctly included as initial sum.
11. `test_rollback_journal_checksum_512_page` -- For 512-byte page: verify correct sample count and offsets (stride 200, offsets 312, 112; count=2).
12. `test_rollback_journal_checksum_round_trip` -- Compute checksum for known page data, store, re-validate; matches.

- test_e2e_bd_2fas_compliance: concrete E2E identifier for structure lint compliance.

Create a WAL-mode database, perform several commits (creating a multi-frame WAL), intentionally corrupt one frame's checksum, then open the database and verify recovery replays only the valid prefix. Similarly, create a rollback-journal mode database, perform a transaction, verify journal checksum computation, simulate crash recovery from hot journal.

## ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA

- WAL checksum chain correctly seeds and chains across header and all frames.
- WAL recovery correctly identifies the valid frame prefix by checksum AND salt matching.
- Corrupt frame terminates recovery at the correct boundary (no data loss beyond the corrupt frame).
- Rollback journal stride-200 checksum matches C SQLite's pager_cksum() exactly.
- data[0] is NEVER sampled in rollback journal checksum.
- Cross-compatibility: WAL files created by C SQLite are recoverable by FrankenSQLite and vice versa.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:42:56.615098307Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T19:14:14.562761658Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T19:14:14.562741179Z","close_reason":"Completed WAL checksum-chain and rollback stride-200 compliance harness with passing gates","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2fas","depends_on_id":"bd-294","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:49:19.556073554Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2fas","depends_on_id":"bd-bca.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-08T09:38:21.014307792Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":192,"issue_id":"bd-2fas","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# §11.9 WAL Checksum Chain Recovery + Rollback Journal Stride-200 Checksum

## Spec Reference
COMPREHENSIVE_SPEC_FOR_FRANKENSQLITE_V1.md lines 13958-13977 (§11.9.1) and
lines 14099-14134 (§11.14)

## Scope

Validate two distinct checksum algorithms used in FrankenSQLite:

1. **WAL checksum chain:** Double-accumulator checksum chained across WAL header
   and frames, used for crash recovery to determine the valid prefix of the WAL.

2. **Rollback journal checksum:** Nonce-seeded stride-200 sampling checksum for
   rollback journal page records, with the critical invariant that `data[0]` is
   NEVER sampled.

## WAL Checksum Chain (§11.9.1)

The WAL uses a custom double-accumulator checksum (NOT CRC-32, NOT xxHash).

**Chain structure:**
1. WAL header checksum: `wal_checksum(header_bytes[0..24], 0, 0, big_end_cksum)`
   → stored at header bytes 24..32
2. First frame: `wal_checksum(frame_header[0..8] ++ page_data, hdr_cksum1, hdr_cksum2, big_end_cksum)`
   → stored at frame header bytes 16..24
   NOTE: Only first 8 bytes of frame header checksummed (NOT bytes 8..16 = salt)
3. Subsequent frames: use previous frame's (cksum1, cksum2) as seed.
   Each frame's checksum covers itself AND all prior frames (cumulative).

**Validation during recovery:** Walk frames sequentially. A frame is valid iff:
- Recomputed checksum matches stored values, AND
- Salt matches the WAL header salt.
First failing frame terminates the valid prefix.

## Rollback Journal Checksum (§11.14)

```
checksum = nonce + data[page_size-200] + data[page_size-400] + ... + data[k]
```

Where `k` is the smallest value > 0 in the arithmetic sequence.

**Critical invariant:** `data[0]` is NEVER sampled. The loop condition is
`while( i > 0 )`, so it stops before reaching index 0.

Each `data[i]` reads a single u8 byte, accumulated into a u32 sum.

**Example for 4096-byte pages:**
- Offsets sampled: 3896, 3696, 3496, ..., 296, 96
- Count: (3896 - 96) / 200 + 1 = 20 bytes sampled
- data[0] NOT included

## Unit Test Specifications

### Test 1: WAL header checksum
Create a 24-byte WAL header. Compute checksum with seed (0, 0).
Verify output matches known C SQLite values for the same header bytes.

### Test 2: WAL first frame checksum chain
Compute header checksum → use as seed for first frame.
Frame input = frame_header[0..8] ++ page_data (NOT including salt bytes 8..16).
Verify the chain produces correct (cksum1, cksum2).

### Test 3: WAL multi-frame chain — cumulative property
Compute 3 frames in chain. Verify frame 3's checksum is seeded by frame 2's
output, which was seeded by frame 1's output, which was seeded by header's.
Corrupt frame 2's stored checksum — verify frame 3's recomputation detects it.

### Test 4: WAL recovery — valid prefix detection
Create a WAL with 5 frames. Corrupt frame 3's checksum. Walk the chain.
Verify recovery returns frames 1-2 as valid, frame 3 onward as invalid.

### Test 5: WAL recovery — salt mismatch terminates
Create a WAL with 3 valid frames (checksums correct). Change salt in frame 2.
Verify recovery stops at frame 2 even though checksum chain is intact.

### Test 6: WAL frame checksum excludes salt bytes
Verify that changing bytes 8..16 of the frame header (salt) does NOT change
the frame's checksum. Only bytes 0..8 of frame header are checksummed.

### Test 7: Rollback journal checksum — stride-200 sampling
For a 4096-byte page with nonce=42:
- Manually compute: 42 + data[3896] + data[3696] + ... + data[96]
- Verify our implementation matches
- Count exactly 20 bytes sampled

### Test 8: Rollback journal checksum — data[0] never sampled
Create a page where data[0] = 0xFF and all other bytes = 0x00.
Compute checksum. Verify it equals just the nonce (since all sampled bytes
are 0x00). Changing data[0] must NOT change the checksum.

### Test 9: Rollback journal checksum — different page sizes
Test with page sizes 512, 1024, 2048, 4096, 8192, 16384, 32768, 65536.
For each, verify:
- The stride is always 200
- data[0] is never sampled
- The number of samples = ceil((page_size - 200) / 200) if page_size > 200
  (verify exact count for each size)

### Test 10: Rollback journal checksum — nonce contribution
Verify nonce is added once (not per-sample). checksum(nonce=0, page) + 42
== checksum(nonce=42, page) for any page content.

### Test 11: Endianness — WAL big_end_cksum flag
Test WAL checksum with big_end_cksum=true and big_end_cksum=false.
Verify they produce DIFFERENT results for the same input.
Verify the flag is read from WAL header byte and used consistently.

## Acceptance Criteria
- WAL checksum chain correctly seeds each frame from the previous
- WAL recovery detects corrupted frames and salt mismatches
- Frame checksum covers only frame_header[0..8], NOT salt bytes 8..16
- Rollback journal checksum uses stride-200 sampling
- data[0] is NEVER sampled in rollback journal checksum (critical invariant)
- Nonce is added exactly once to rollback journal checksum
- Both algorithms produce results matching C SQLite for known test vectors
- All tests pass under `cargo test`
","created_at":"2026-02-08T06:48:08Z"},{"id":353,"issue_id":"bd-2fas","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## E2E Test

- **test_e2e_wal_checksum_chain_recovery_integration**:
  - Create a DB, run multiple transactions to populate WAL.
  - Corrupt a mid-WAL frame checksum and verify recovery truncates at the first invalid frame (SQLite semantics) unless WAL-FEC is enabled.
  - Also validate rollback journal checksum behavior under corruption.

## Logging Requirements

- INFO: recovery walk: `frame_no`, `valid_prefix_end`, `reason` (checksum_mismatch|salt_mismatch|eof).
- DEBUG: checksum recomputation details (throttled): `cksum1`, `cksum2`, `big_end_cksum`.
- ERROR: rollback journal checksum mismatch: `page_no`, `nonce`, `sample_count`.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] E2E identifier for this bead is concrete and executable.
- [ ] Logging requirements include DEBUG/INFO/WARN/ERROR and bd-1fpm alignment.","created_at":"2026-02-09T03:10:13Z"},{"id":945,"issue_id":"bd-2fas","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"RoseHollow implementation complete in crates/fsqlite-harness/tests/bd_2fas_wal_checksum_chain_recovery_compliance.rs.  {"id":"bd-2gn9","title":"OpLog Generator: Seeded concurrent write workloads (commutative + contended)","description":"## Goal
Implement a generator that emits OpLogs for selected fixtures/presets.

## Design Notes
- Prefer **structured ops** -> render SQL late so both executors use identical SQL.
- Use commutative operations for deterministic final logical state (disjoint key ranges / disjoint tables).
- For contended presets, specify retry policy (backoff, max retries) so executors behave comparably.

## Acceptance
- `--seed` produces identical OpLog bytes across runs.
- Can generate both:
  - commutative workload (expected to converge without conflicts)
  - contended workload (expected to trigger busy/retry)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:19:53.776699576Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.251719265Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T04:40:53.251705649Z","close_reason":"Duplicate of existing E2E suite tasks; superseded by canonical bd-z0xm subgraph.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","workload"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2gn9","depends_on_id":"bd-1an0","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-10T04:20:56.315349069Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1045,"issue_id":"bd-2gn9","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"## Acceptance Criteria
